thread.title: Shaun Woods takes oath as new Tyngsboro police chief
thread.title_full: Shaun Woods takes oath as new Tyngsboro police chief

author: Aaron Curtis
title: Shaun Woods takes oath as new Tyngsboro police chief
text:
News Crime and Public Safety Crime and Public Safety | Shaun Woods takes oath as new Tyngsboro police chief Woods sworn in at Town Hall ceremony on Monday Share this: Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Click to share on X (Opens in new window)
Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods, pinned by his wife, Elizabeth, takes the helm after officially being sworn in at Town Hall on Nov. 18, 2024. Woods succeeds Chief Richard Howe, who retired in Oct. 2024, following 29 years of service in Tyngsboro. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
At his swearing-in ceremony on Nov. 18, 2024, Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods shares a celebratory fist bump with his daughter, Callie, at Town Hall. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods delivers a speech during his swearing-in ceremony at Town Hall on Nov. 18, 2024. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods stands with Town Manager Colin Loiselle at his swearing-in ceremony on Nov. 18, 2024. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
Newly sworn-in Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods stands with fellow department members at Town Hall on Nov. 18, 2024. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
Show Caption 1 of 5 Tyngsboro Police Chief Shaun Woods, pinned by his wife, Elizabeth, takes the helm after officially being sworn in at Town Hall on Nov. 18, 2024. Woods succeeds Chief Richard Howe, who retired in Oct. 2024, following 29 years of service in Tyngsboro. (Courtesy Tyngsboro Police Department)
Expand By Aaron Curtis | acurtis@lowellsun.com | Lowell Sun PUBLISHED: November 22, 2024 at 5:09 PM EST TYNGSBORO — Shaun Woods has officially taken the helm as Tyngsboro’s new police chief following a swearing-in ceremony at Town Hall on Monday.
“Anyone who knows me knows how much this community means to me,” Woods said during the event held in front of his family, friends and colleagues. “As someone who lives here, and who is raising my family here, no one has a greater vested interest in Tyngsboro’s safety and well-being than I do. I promise you I am deeply committed to ensuring that our town remains a safe and welcoming place for all, and I promise to work tirelessly to protect it.”
Town Manager Colin Loiselle announced Woods’ appointment on Oct. 15, following a search process that began before the retirement of former Police Chief Richard Howe at the end of September, after a 29-year career in Tyngsboro.
Loiselle expressed pride in naming Woods the next chief, describing him as a dedicated volunteer, youth sports coach, active Rotary Club member, and a presence at nearly every town event.
“He has done every job, every task, and every function within the Tyngsboro Police Department including shoveling, taking out the trash, and much more,” Loiselle said. “I am incredibly proud to name Shaun Woods our next chief of police and am confident that under his leadership the Tyngsboro Police Department will continue to be the first-class law enforcement agency that this community deserves.”
Woods began his public safety career as a dispatcher and civilian attendant for Lowell in 1999. He served as a dispatcher in Tyngsboro from 2000 to 2001 before entering the Lowell Police Academy. When a patrol officer position opened in Tyngsboro, he left the academy to work for Tyngsboro full-time.
Woods, who holds a bachelor’s degree in criminology and a master’s in criminal justice, has been with the Tyngsboro Police for approximately 24 years. He has served in every rank within the department, including six years as sergeant, seven years as lieutenant, and most recently six years as deputy chief.
Woods served as the acting chief until his official appointment earlier this month.
During his address on Monday, Woods thanked Loiselle and the Select Board for trusting him to oversee the safety of the community, a responsibility he said he takes “with the utmost seriousness.” He also expressed gratitude to his fellow department members for their dedication and commitment.
“I am committed to upholding our department’s mission of building trust, fostering partnerships, and addressing the quality-of-life issues that matter most to you,” Woods said. “I pledge to listen to your concerns and to lead with professionalism, fairness, and integrity — to ensure that the Tyngsborough Police Department remains a source of pride for our town.”
Follow Aaron Curtis on X, formerly known as Twitter, @aselahcurtis
